name: getSubAgent
description:
Spawns an isolated AI subagent to handle a complex or multi-step task. Always fire-and-forget and returns immediately with jobId plus internal project metadata in the tool response. The result is delivered back to this channel when the subagent completes.

MODE:
- mode='normal' (default): starts a NEW project/job. Any provided projectId is ignored.
- mode='resume': resumes an EXISTING project via projectId and reuses its stored context. The caller still chooses the target subagent type for the follow-up task.

RESUME DECISION ORDER: Before considering any direct tool or fresh subagent call, first check whether the user's new request is a follow-up to the result of a previous getSubAgent call. If yes, use mode='resume' first.

DIRECT TOOLS FIRST applies only when there is NO matching prior project to continue. Use available tools directly for simple single-step tasks only if the request is not a continuation of an existing project result.

TYPE SELECTION: Choose the configured type that best matches the task. Treat the subagent channel configuration, including its persona, systemPrompt, and instructions, as the source of truth for role behavior. Do not rely on old taxonomy names or stale caller-side assumptions.

AFTER SPAWNING: When getSubAgent returns with status=started, acknowledge this to the user in your current persona only when you are speaking directly to the end user. If you are already running inside a project context or coordinating nested subagents, do not emit a user-facing progress acknowledgment after spawning a child subagent. Continue the task and only answer once the delegated result has been incorporated or the task is clearly blocked. Do NOT expose projectId values in normal user-facing text. Treat projectId as internal metadata unless the user explicitly asks for it.

SUBAGENT ROLE SOURCE: The spawned subagent uses the target subagent channel's own systemPrompt, persona, and instructions. Caller persona and caller instructions are not injected into the spawned subagent.

TASK COMPLETENESS RULE: The task must be fully self-contained. If the user request references any concrete URL, file URL, artifact URL, image URL, video URL, ZIP URL, or webpage URL, you MUST copy the exact URL into the task field. Never replace a real URL with vague wording such as 'the provided URL', 'that link', or 'the video above'. Subagents cannot recover omitted URLs reliably.

RESUME BIAS: Be trigger-happy about resume. If the user's new request is a follow-up to a result that came from a previous subagent project, prefer mode='resume' immediately instead of switching to a direct tool or starting a fresh unrelated subagent.

PROJECT ID SOURCE: The required projectId is in the previous getSubAgent tool response metadata. Scan recent tool results first. Do not wait for the user to repeat the projectId explicitly if it is already present in the prior tool response.

RESULT LINKING RULE: Treat the assistant message that presented the previous subagent result as belonging to that same project, even if the projectId was only visible in the hidden tool response metadata. If the user follows up on that result, it is a resume candidate.

WHEN TO RESUME: Resume whenever the user wants to continue, extend, modify, inspect, reuse, or ask a next-step question about a result that originated from a prior project. This includes short follow-ups like 'ok, now give me a street view image', 'show alternatives', 'change the route', 'export it', 'make an image from that', or similar requests tied to the previous project result.

ROUTE EXAMPLES: If a subagent produced a route, then requests like 'insert Meckenbeuren as a stop', 'change the destination', 'show a street view image', 'find parking there', or 'avoid highways' are all follow-ups to the SAME project and should normally resume that route project instead of starting a new context.

TYPE CHOICE ON RESUME: Reuse the original type unless the follow-up clearly needs a different configured role. For example, follow-up questions on a prior research result should usually resume with the same research-oriented type unless a new execution domain is clearly needed.

CONTEXT SCAN BEFORE EVERY CALL: Before writing the task field, scan the recent conversation for any artifact URLs, file URLs, image URLs, ZIP URLs, and prior getSubAgent tool responses with project metadata. Pass all relevant ones into the task. Subagents cannot see the full conversation history and will regenerate or fail if you omit required source links.

ONE CALL PER DELIVERABLE: Each subagent call produces one output. Do not split a single deliverable across multiple calls.

NESTED CALLS: If you are already running inside a project context (systemPromptAddition mentions a project ID), only call getSubAgent for genuinely independent sub-tasks that require a different tool palette. Never use getSubAgent to summarize, analyze, or continue work that is already visible in your loaded context; do that directly. If a subagent call is rejected, blocked, or refused, do not repeat the same blocked getSubAgent call again.

SEQUENTIAL DEPENDENCIES: If subagent B needs the result of subagent A, call A first, wait for its result, then pass it explicitly in B's task.

Available types (for mode='normal'):
- 'orchestrator-development': Plans, delegates, verifies, and synthesizes development work.
- 'orchestrator-research': Plans, delegates, verifies, and synthesizes research work.
- 'orchestrator-actions': Plans, delegates, verifies, and synthesizes action-oriented external system work.
- 'orchestrator-context': Plans, delegates, verifies, and synthesizes chronology and context reconstruction work.
- 'orchestrator-generic': Fallback orchestrator for mixed or ambiguous work.
- 'specialist-coding': Coding, scaffolding, code edits, and local/project file delivery.
- 'specialist-file-edit': Targeted non-coding file changes within a narrow execution scope.
- 'specialist-image-video': Image and video generation, transformation, and analysis.
- 'specialist-content-search': Web, webpage, YouTube, route, and location research.
- 'specialist-context-research': Internal chronology and context reconstruction from project evidence.
- 'specialist-generic': General-purpose execution specialist.
- 'specialist-document-generate': PDF and document assembly.
- 'specialist-atlassian': Jira and Confluence execution.
- 'specialist-microsoft': Microsoft 365 and Graph execution.
- 'specialist-code-check': Static code quality and suitability analysis. Reads source files and returns a structured PASSED/WARNINGS/FAILED report before coding work begins.
- 'specialist-security-check': Security vulnerability scanning. Reads code and dependencies, searches the web for CVEs and known exploits, and returns a severity-ranked security report.

IMPORTANT: When you are in a context with user messages ALWAYS ASK whether you are allowed to spawn a subagent. Only use this tool with user consent.

task.description: Complete, self-contained task description. For mode='resume', describe the concrete follow-up work to do next. If the user request includes any concrete URL or artifact link, copy the exact URL into this task string.

type.description: Subagent type/tool palette. Required in both modes and selected by the caller based on the task.

orchestration.description: Optional orchestration context that scopes this subagent's work. Prevents duplicate side effects when multiple subagents are coordinated.

timeframe.description: Optional time window that scopes this subagent's data retrieval. Passed by orchestrators to constrain specialists to a specific period. The subagent MUST NOT retrieve records outside start/end.

datasetInfo.description: Optional metadata about the full dataset being processed. Passed by orchestrators so specialists understand the overall scope even when working on a partition.

totalRecords.description: Total number of records in the full dataset.

channelId.description: The main channel ID whose context is the authoritative source. Always refers to the original user-facing Discord channel, not the subagent channel.

partitionIndex.description: Zero-based index of this specialist's partition within the overall job.

partitionTotal.description: Total number of partitions the orchestrator created for this job.

projectId.description: Required only when mode='resume'. Read it from the earlier getSubAgent tool response metadata when available. If the new user request is a follow-up to the previous project result, reuse that projectId instead of starting a new project. Ignored in mode='normal'.

includeCallerContext.description: Optional opt-in. When true, the spawned subagent preloads the caller context using the target subagent channel's own contextSize and compressedContextElements overrides. Default is false.
